steps.title: Identify the uncertainty
steps.content: The passage shows that Bellamy's claimed authorship of the pledge (August 1892) is now contradicted by evidence of the pledge existing earlier. The passage explicitly concludes: “Bellamy may not have created the pledge after all.” The uncertain piece of knowledge is who actually wrote the pledge first.

steps.title: Select the matching choice
steps.content: Choice D—“The identity of the person who first wrote the pledge”—captures exactly what's uncertain: the original author. Choices A and B address facts not discussed (changes in the 1900s, student preferences), and choice C mentions something in the passage but tangential to what's uncertain (the newspaper's name). That matches choice D — D is correct.
